steps.title: Identify what the blank must mean
steps.content: The sentence says: "we are unlikely to ever have ______ account of Hatshepsut's reign because much of the evidence was deliberately destroyed." Destroyed evidence makes it impossible to produce a thorough, complete account — the blank must mean comprehensive or exhaustive.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'a superficial': Means shallow or lacking depth. Destroyed evidence would not prevent a superficial account; you can still write one with limited information. ✗
  • Choice B — 'an imaginative': Means creative or inventive. Destroying evidence does not prevent speculation or imagination — it prevents factual completeness. ✗
  • Choice C — 'a questionable': Means doubtful or unreliable. Paradoxically, destroyed evidence might make us more likely to produce a questionable account, not less likely. ✗
  • Choice D — 'an exhaustive': Means thorough and complete in every detail. This is precisely what destroyed evidence prevents us from ever having. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
